title: More Nonsense Limerick 91
content:
There was an old man, who when little
    Fell casually into a kettle;
    But, growing too stout, 
    He could never get out,
    So he passed all his life in that kettle.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Lear delivers a powerful performance in "More Nonsense Limerick 91"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
